title: psephellus poluninii
intro: Psephellus poluninii, also known as the Polunin's mountain daisy or mountain wormwood, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Tibetan medicine. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Psephellus poluninii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections.
